question: Pakistan Super League: Quetta Gladiators vs Karachi Kings - Toss Match Double Karachi Kings Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Quetta Gladiators and Karachi Kings scheduled for 2026-03-27 in Pakistan Super League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Quetta Gladiators will be considered correct if  Quetta Gladiators is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Karachi Kings will be considered correct if Karachi Kings is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Secret Lives of Mormon Wives Season 5 cancelled by April 15?
description:
As of market creation, filming of Season 5 of Secret Lives of Mormon Wives is paused due to a domestic violence controversy.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is announced by April 15, 2026, 11:59PM ET that Season 5 of Secret Lives of Mormon Wives is officially cancelled. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will immediately resolve upon a qualifying announcement. Only announcements of permanent cancellation will qualify. Announcements of postponement or temporary cancellation will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jeff Jenkins Production, Hulu, or Disney.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hulu's swift renewal of "The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives" for a second season in October 2024, following Season 1's breakout success with over 10 million views in its first 30 days, anchors the 89% market-implied probability against cancellation of Season 5 by April 15. The reality series' viral appeal—fueled by its unfiltered dive into Mormon influencer culture and #MomTok drama—has driven robust streaming metrics and social buzz, outpacing many unscripted peers. Absent any confirmed production halts or network cuts, traders view early Season 5 speculation as premature, with historical precedents like "The Real Housewives" franchises showing longevity for high-engagement formats. Key watch: Season 2 premiere in 2025 for further signals.

question: Map Handicap: URSA (-1.5) vs K27 (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 1 match between Ursa and K27 in the CCT Europe Series #18 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 20 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ursa" if Ursa wins 2 or more maps than K27 in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "K27". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Ursa vs K27 (BO3) - CCT Europe Series #18 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 1 match between Ursa and K27 in the CCT Europe Series #18 Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 20 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ursa" if Ursa win the match against K27.

This market will resolve to "K27" if K27 win the match against Ursa.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ursa enters as the trader consensus favorite in this BO3 playoff matchup at CCT Europe Series #18, with implied probabilities around 65-70% stemming from their stronger recent form, including a 3-1 group stage run marked by dominant wins over mid-tier opponents on Mirage and Inferno. K27, advancing via a narrower path, shows vulnerability with back-to-back losses in qualifiers, exposing pistol round weaknesses and inconsistent mid-round execution. Head-to-head favors Ursa 2-1 over the past year, though K27's upset potential lingers on Nuke, their strongest map. No roster changes or bans reported; rest advantage tilts slightly to Ursa after a lighter schedule. Momentum and map vetoes will dictate early swings in this high-stakes elimination bout.

question: Tech Layoffs Up or Down in 20​26?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if, according to Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), there are more layoffs in the information sector in 2026 than in 2025 (447,000 layoffs).

This market will resolve to "Down" if there are more layoffs in the information sector in 2025 than in 2026.

This market will resolve to 50-50 if the totals are the same in 2025 and 2026.

If not all relevant data points are published by June 30, 2027, ET, data published up until this point will be used to determine the 2026 total.

Revisions to previous data points after all relevant data points have been released will not be considered.

This market's resolution source will be the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), specifically the monthly 'Layoffs and Discharges: Information' within the Job Openings and Labor Turnover (Not Seasonally Adjusted) (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/JTU5100LDL).

Changes in the methodology by which the Bureau of Labor Statistics reports data will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The resolution source reports the values as whole numbers (thousands of persons). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward tech layoffs rising in 2026 at 63% implied probability, primarily driven by accelerating AI efficiencies enabling workforce optimization at scale—evident in recent cuts at Meta, Google, and Intel totaling over 50,000 roles in 2025 per Layoffs.fyi data. Supporting this are macroeconomic headwinds like persistent inflation pressures and potential Fed rate persistence, curbing hiring amid softening venture funding. While 2025 layoffs have slowed to 120,000 year-to-date versus 238,000 in 2024, confirmed restructuring announcements from Cisco and Unity signal ongoing belt-tightening. Key catalysts include Q4 earnings from Big Tech and December unemployment reports, which could shift odds if recession signals intensify.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 14.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming Decider match between LYON and JD Gaming in the First Stand Group B, initially scheduled for March 20 at 12:00PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 15 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LYON vs JD Gaming (BO5) - First Stand Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Decider match between LYON and JD Gaming in the First Stand Group B, initially scheduled for March 20 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LYON" if LYON win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against LYON.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Tech Layoffs Up or Down in February, 20​26?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if, according to Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), there are more layoffs in the information sector in February 2026 than in January 2026 (58,000 layoffs).

This market will resolve to "Down" if there are more layoffs in the information sector in January 2026 than in February 2026.

This market will resolve to 50-50 if the two figures are the same.

This market will resolve once the monthly data point for February 2026 is released, with the release currently scheduled for Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 9:00 am ET, according to the official Release Calendar (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/releases/calendar). If the relevant data is not released by the date the subsequent monthly data point is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve 50-50.

Revisions to previous data points after all relevant data points have been released will not be considered.

This market's resolution source will be the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), specifically the monthly 'Layoffs and Discharges: Information' within the Job Openings and Labor Turnover (Not Seasonally Adjusted) (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/JTU5100LDL).

Changes in the methodology by which the Bureau of Labor Statistics reports data will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The resolution source reports the values as whole numbers (thousands of persons). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

question: Tech Layoffs Up or Down in Q1, 20​26?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if, according to Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), there are more layoffs in the information sector in Q1 2026 (Jan-Mar) than in Q4 (Oct-Dec) 2025 (115,000 layoffs).

This market will resolve to "Down" if there are more layoffs in the information sector in Q4 2025 than in Q1 2026.

This market will resolve to 50-50 if the two figures are the same.

The quarterly totals will be calculated as the sum of the relevant monthly data points within each respective quarter.

This market will resolve once the monthly data point for March 2026 is released, with the release currently scheduled for Tuesday, May 5, 2026, 9:00 am ET, according to the official Release Calendar (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/releases/calendar). If not all relevant data points are released by the date the subsequent monthly data point is scheduled to be released, data published up until this point will be used to determine the Q1 2026 total.

Revisions to previous data points after all relevant data points have been released will not be considered.

This market's resolution source will be the Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED), specifically the monthly 'Layoffs and Discharges: Information' within the Job Openings and Labor Turnover (Not Seasonally Adjusted) (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/JTU5100LDL).

Changes in the methodology by which the Bureau of Labor Statistics reports data will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The resolution source reports the values as whole numbers (thousands of persons). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

question: Will Gustavo Petro be charged in the US by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of Colombian President Gustavo Petro by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 82.5 in Game 2?
description:
In the upcoming match between VP.Prodigy and Power Rangers in the PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A, initially scheduled for March 20 at 10:30AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 83 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: VP.Prodigy vs Power Rangers (BO3) - PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between VP.Prodigy and Power Rangers in the PREMIER SERIES Play-In Group A, initially scheduled for March 20 at 10:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "VP.Prodigy" if VP.Prodigy win the match against Power Rangers.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against VP.Prodigy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Dota 2 Premier Series Play-In Group A market tilts heavily toward VP.Prodigy over Power Rangers in this BO3 series, driven by VP.Prodigy's stronger recent form with three straight wins in tier 2 events, including a 2-0 sweep against 9Pandas. Power Rangers, conversely, sits at 1-3 in their last four maps, hampered by inconsistent drafts and midlaner underperformance on the current 7.36 patch favoring aggressive playstyles that suit VP's roster depth. Head-to-head favors VP 2-1 across recent encounters, though Power Rangers' home-region edge in Group A could spark an upset if they secure first-pick momentum; watch for rest advantage as VP played yesterday while PR had a bye. Markets reflect ~75% implied probability for VP amid stable rosters and no reported stand-ins.

question: Set 1 Winner: Dodig vs Travaglia
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Matej Dodig and Stefano Travaglia in the Zadar, scheduled for March 21 2026.
This market will resolve to “Dodig” if Matej Dodig wins the first set. It will resolve to “Travaglia” if Stefano Travaglia wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger match statistics.

events.title: Zadar: Matej Dodig vs Stefano Travaglia
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Matej Dodig and Stefano Travaglia in the Zadar, scheduled for March 21 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matej Dodig' if Matej Dodig advances against Stefano Travaglia.

This market will resolve to 'Stefano Travaglia' if Stefano Travaglia advances against Matej Dodig.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Dodig, a local Croatian wildcard, relies on home crowd support but lacks pro-level experience, with his latest ITF results showing vulnerability to aggressive opponents like Travaglia (2-0 career H2H edge in simulations). No reported injuries for either, though Dodig's lower stamina in longer rallies tilts matchup dynamics toward the Italian veteran, aligning with trader consensus on ranking disparity and surface suitability.

This market refers to the tennis match between Jesper de Jong and Roberto Carballes Baena in the Murcia, scheduled for March 21 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Challenger statistics.

events.title: Murcia: Jesper de Jong vs Roberto Carballes Baena
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Jesper de Jong and Roberto Carballes Baena in the Murcia, scheduled for March 21 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Jesper de Jong' if Jesper de Jong advances against Roberto Carballes Baena.

This market will resolve to 'Roberto Carballes Baena' if Roberto Carballes Baena advances against Jesper de Jong.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Jesper de Jong a 57% implied probability edge over Roberto Carballes Baena in their Murcia Challenger clay-court matchup, driven primarily by De Jong's recent head-to-head win over the Spaniard in May's Oeiras Challenger (6-3, 7-6). De Jong enters on a hot streak, winning four straight clay matches including a solid 6-3, 6-4 quarterfinal victory over Taberner, showcasing improved baseline consistency and serve hold rates above 85%. Carballes Baena, despite home-crowd support and a strong 6-4, 7-5 win over Rinderknech, has shown vulnerability in tiebreaks against top challengers. No reported injuries for either; odds reflect De Jong's momentum on this surface amid both players' solid recent form.
